novel_features.name: Roadmap-aware next-video picker
novel_features.description: Get the next unwatched videos tuned to where you are on the fluency roadmap, sorted by fine-grained difficulty.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining your cumulative-hours level to the cached catalog minus your watched playlist, ranked by the 1-100 difficulty rating - the app can filter but never picks 'the next video for where you are.'
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this instead of scrolling the catalog when an agent needs the single right-level, not-yet-watched video to recommend.

novel_features.name: CSV bulk external-hours import
novel_features.description: Import a backlog of outside listening/watching hours from a CSV in one command instead of dozens of web-form clicks.
novel_features.rationale: The platform logs external time one entry at a time with no bulk path; only a local CSV-to-batched-POST loop closes the backlog.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this whenever a learner has weeks of un-logged podcast/Netflix time to reconcile in bulk.

novel_features.name: Difficulty-progression readiness analysis
novel_features.description: See whether the difficulty of what you actually watch is trending up over a window - the real signal that you're ready to level up.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining the watched playlist to per-video 1-100 difficulty and the daily series; no community tool analyzes the numeric difficulty of consumed content.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to answer 'is this learner actually progressing, or stuck rewatching easy content?'

novel_features.name: Unified fluency-ladder roadmap
novel_features.description: The whole L1-L7 ladder with comprehensible-input advisories (speaking, reading, native milestones) and personalized calendar ETAs in one view.
novel_features.rationale: Combines cumulative hours, the static level/advisory map, and local pace to compute per-level ETAs; community tools only project a single next milestone.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the question is 'where am I on the path to fluency and when do I hit the next milestone?'

novel_features.name: Caption / transcript fetch
novel_features.description: Fetch a video's captions as clean, timestamp-free plain text (and cache the cue-level transcript).
novel_features.rationale: The /video endpoint returns the full inline WEBVTT, so we clean it and cache it in SQLite - no tool reads captions via the API (the lone extension does fragile player interception).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to read or summarize what a single video actually says rather than just its title and tags.

novel_features.name: Corpus concordance (KWIC) search over all transcripts
novel_features.description: Search every cached transcript for a word, phrase, regex, or verb-tense pattern and get each hit in context with the video, level, guide, and timestamp.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local cue-level transcript corpus joined to catalog metadata plus morphology presets - turning the curated comprehensible-input library into a grammar-aware concordance no language tool offers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find real, level-appropriate examples of a word or grammar form in context - the core ritual of grammar-in-context study.

narrative.headline: Every Dreaming Spanish and French tracking feature in one offline-first, agent-native CLI: roadmap math, transcript concordance, and bulk hour-logging.
narrative.value_prop: Dreaming's web app shows your hours but won't plan, search, or bulk-import for you, and the community's features are scattered across a dozen browser extensions and scripts. This CLI unifies them into one agent-native tool backed by a local SQLite mirror of your catalog, daily series, and external-hours log - so `next` picks your next video offline, `external import` clears a CSV backlog in one shot, and `roadmap` lays out the whole L1-L7 fluency ladder with personalized ETAs.
narrative.auth_narrative: Dreaming has no official API. Authenticate with your account bearer token: copy it from your browser's localStorage 'token' key on app.dreaming.com (run `dreaming auth setup` for the steps), then run `dreaming auth set-token <token>` or set DREAMING_TOKEN. You can also exchange email/password for a token with `sign-in`. Tokens expire periodically - re-run `auth set-token` (or re-export DREAMING_TOKEN) when you hit a 401.

quickstart.comment: Print how to get your bearer token; then `auth set-token <token>` or export DREAMING_TOKEN. Every endpoint is 401 without it.
Pull your catalog, stats, and history into the local store for offline search and joins.
See your position on the L1-L7 fluency ladder with milestone ETAs.
Get the next unwatched videos at your current level.
See your total hours, streak, and recent pace at a glance.

troubleshoots.fix: Your token expired - re-run `dreaming auth set-token <token>` or re-export DREAMING_TOKEN (the token lives in app.dreaming.com localStorage key 'token').
Select the catalog with `--language fr` or set DREAMING_LANGUAGE=fr (default es).

troubleshoots.symptom: 404 or connection errors after a Dreaming update
troubleshoots.fix: The backend host may have moved; set DREAMING_BASE_URL to the new .netlify/functions base.

narrative.when_to_use: Use the Dreaming CLI for any task about a learner's comprehensible-input progress on Dreaming Spanish or French: checking hours and pace toward a fluency level, picking the next right-level video, searching the catalog by topic/guide/dialect, logging or bulk-importing external listening time, and analyzing whether consumed difficulty is trending up. It is the right tool whenever the question involves the hours-based roadmap or the leveled video catalog.

recipes.title: Plan to a deadline
recipes.explanation: Back-solves the daily minutes you need to reach 600 hours (L5, speaking recommended) by a date.

recipes.title: Find the next video, agent-friendly
recipes.explanation: Returns the next unwatched, level-appropriate videos as structured JSON for an agent to recommend.

recipes.title: Narrow a large catalog payload
recipes.explanation: The picker returns verbose video records; --select trims each to just the fields an agent needs.

recipes.title: Break down watched hours by teacher
recipes.explanation: Aggregates your watched videos per guide from the local store as structured JSON.

recipes.title: Check readiness to level up
recipes.explanation: Shows whether the difficulty of what you actually watched is climbing over the last 90 days.

recipes.title: Read a video's transcript
recipes.explanation: Fetches the inline captions and prints clean, timestamp-free text an agent can read or summarize.

recipes.title: Resolve a downloadable stream
recipes.explanation: Prints the signed HLS URL and a ready ffmpeg command; add --exec to download when ffmpeg is installed.

recipes.title: Find a verb tense in real content
recipes.explanation: After 'transcript sync', returns level-appropriate sentences using the imperfect subjunctive, each with its video and timestamp.

recipes.title: Concordance a specific word
recipes.explanation: Keyword-in-context hits for a word across the whole corpus as structured JSON for an agent.

trigger_phrases: what should I watch next on dreaming
how many hours until I can speak spanish
find examples of the subjunctive in dreaming videos
search dreaming transcripts for a word
am I on pace for 600 hours
